title: Research Obsidian.md live autocomplete options
scope: Find and evaluate Obsidian.md options for live inline/tab-style autocomplete or closest equivalents using GPT, Claude, OpenAI-compatible APIs, local models, or subscription-based access. Include official Obsidian plugin listings, GitHub repositories, and maintenance status.
goals: Determine whether true Copilot-like live inline suggestions exist in Obsidian plugins.
Classify every option by interaction mode: true_inline_tab, inline_not_tab, completion_popup, command_or_selection_generation, chat_or_sidebar, custom_only, or not_applicable.
Research plugins such as Obsidian Copilot, Text Generator, Smart Composer, Local GPT/Ollama-related plugins, and any dedicated autocomplete plugins.
Identify setup complexity, UX gaps versus VS Code inline suggestions, privacy posture, and cost model.
Flag plugin API limitations or editor-mode constraints if relevant.
non_goals: Do not research VS Code except for cross-editor projects.
Do not write the final report.
safety: Do not read tmp/, archive/, or prompt-buffer.md.
Do not edit files tagged human-written.
Use current web sources for plugin status.
success_criteria: Include a ranked option list with evidence, pros, cons, and confidence.
Explicitly separate true live tab autocomplete from command/chat/selection-based generation.
